title: County Of San Diego Instructional Service Guide Ssd Commercial
text:

Fo o d  S c ra p s
Cooked or raw meat, poultry and seafood (including 
bones), cheese, dairy products, fruits, vegetables, grains, 
pasta, egg shells, bread, coffee grounds, tea bags, tea 
leaves, baked goods, nuts, jelly, candy, snack foods, 
leftovers, and spoiled food.
How to Collect
• Use a kitchen caddie.
• Empty food scraps directly in your container (loose or in   
paper bags) and ensure the lid closes. Please do not use any 	
kind of plastic bags!
What to Avoid
Do not include liquids, plastic, glass, metals, or Styrofoam.
ALL ORGANICS GO IN ONE CONTAINER
Organic waste (also referred to as organics), which includes food waste, green waste, landscape and 
pruning waste, non-hazardous wood waste, and food-soiled paper waste, will be collected all mixed 
together in your green organics container. If it grows, it goes!
Ya rd Waste
Grass clippings, small branches, small tree limbs, tree 
roots, flowers, leaves, plants, and straw.
How to Collect
• Place yard trimmings directly in your green container 
(loose or in paper bags) and ensure the lid closes.
What to Avoid
No rocks, dirt, soil, tree stumps, or plastic bags.
Food-Soiled Paper
Paper bags, paper napkins, paper towels, paper plates, 
paper cups, paper take-out containers and take-out boxes 
(with no plastic or wax coating, and with metal removed), 
coffee filters, tissues, food-soiled pizza boxes and food-
soiled cardboard boxes. 
How to Collect
• Paper can be dry or wet.
• Place food-soiled paper directly in your container (loose 
or in paper bags) and ensure the lid closes.
Na t u ra l Fib e r s
Popsicle sticks, sawdust, toothpicks, wooden chopsticks, and untreated 
wood.
How to Collect
• Place natural fibers directly in your container (loose or in paper bags) 
and 	ensure the lid closes.
MINIMUM CONTAINER CAPACITY GUIDELINE FOR ORGANIC MATERIAL 
FOR MULTI-FAMILY CUSTOMERS:
